pattern: Once the trailing digits are pinned to a fixed suffix, only the leading digits are free, so counting powerful integers up to a bound reduces to counting digit-limited prefixes up to a derived prefix bound.
hints: Any powerful integer is built from a free leading part followed by the fixed suffix, so split counting-up-to-a-bound into counting valid leading parts.

For a bound with the same number of digits as the suffix, a leading part is valid whenever it is strictly smaller than the bound's own leading part, plus possibly one more if the leading part matches exactly and the suffix itself does not exceed the bound.

Count digit-limited numbers up to a string bound by scanning digit by digit: once a digit position where the bound's digit exceeds the limit is found, every combination of at-most-limit digits fills the remaining positions freely.

edge_inputs.description: several powerful integers share a three-digit suffix
a two-digit suffix with a wide digit limit
the suffix itself never fits inside the range
start equals the suffix value exactly
single-digit suffix at the tightest limit
range and suffix pushed to the top of the constraint space
finish shares every leading digit with the suffix boundary, forcing the exact-match branch
a tight digit limit that rules out most leading-digit combinations

stress.description: random ranges with random suffixes respecting the digit limit
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    limit = rng.randint(1, 9)
    slen = rng.randint(1, 6)
    digits = [str(rng.randint(0, limit)) for _ in range(slen)]
    if digits[0] == '0':
        digits[0] = str(rng.randint(1, limit))
    s = ''.join(digits)
    start = rng.randint(1, 10**12)
    finish = start + rng.randint(0, 10**12)
    return [start, finish, limit, s]
stress.note: wide random coverage of prefix/suffix boundary interactions

stress.description: ranges pushed toward the full 10^15 boundary
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    limit = rng.randint(1, 9)
    slen = rng.randint(1, 4)
    digits = [str(rng.randint(0, limit)) for _ in range(slen)]
    if digits[0] == '0':
        digits[0] = str(rng.randint(1, limit))
    s = ''.join(digits)
    finish = rng.randint(10**14, 10**15)
    start = rng.randint(1, finish)
    return [start, finish, limit, s]
stress.note: exercises the largest prefix lengths the count_le scan can see
